status: exact modular-blend saddle geometry with finite frequency-adaptive diagnostics and a retired monotonicity branch; not a proof of Lambda<=0 or RH
proof_boundary: This artifact proves the exact component saddle and transition-index formulas and records ten high-precision adaptive-truncation diagnostics for both the Laguerre value and its sampled monotonicity margin. The latter global sign is false by the separate Xi Lehmer certificate. It does not prove a contour deformation, a uniform two-index collar, the Laguerre remainder sign, strict Laguerre positivity, Lambda<=0, or RH.

exact.uniform_asymptotic: For 0<=t<=1/2 and a in {5,9}, n_*(a,t,x)=sqrt(x/(4*pi))*(1+O(x^-1)) as x->infinity.
exact.interpretation: The modular switch changes side near Re(u_*)=0. A fixed arithmetic truncation eventually misses the saddle transition; a viable truncation must grow on the Riemann-Siegel scale sqrt(x/(4*pi)).

rows.claim: Each theta component has an explicit deformed complex phase.
At zero Newman time the component saddle is explicit.
rows.proof_boundary: Principal saddle geometry, not a contour theorem.

rows.claim: The modular side transition occurs on the Riemann-Siegel square-root scale.
rows.proof_boundary: Exact saddle threshold; no Laguerre sign follows.

rows.claim: The positive-time transition obeys an exact scalar fixed-point law.
rows.proof_boundary: Saddle crossing only; no steepest-descent remainder is bounded.

rows.claim: Bounded positive Newman time preserves the leading square-root transition scale.
rows.formula: For 0<=t<=1/2 and a in {5,9}, n_*(a,t,x)=sqrt(x/(4*pi))*(1+O(x^-1)) as x->infinity.
rows.proof_boundary: Leading scale only; constants and a collar theorem remain open.

rows.claim: High-precision rows show that the block counts needed for scale-level Laguerre and monotonicity-margin agreement grow together with the saddle index.
rows.formula: N_L=N_M=3,4,4,5,6 at x=80,100,120,150,200 for both sampled times
rows.proof_boundary: Ten point diagnostics only; the exact Lehmer-point counterexample shows that the sampled monotonicity signs are not globally representative.

rows.claim: A two-index collar covers all observed transition rows but is not promoted to either sign theorem.
rows.proof_boundary: Finite empirical collar; no global tail sign is asserted.

rows.claim: The surviving architecture applies the frequency-adaptive modular remainder only to direct Laguerre positivity.
rows.formula: Choose N(x,t) on the saddle scale, retain both a=5 and a=9 transitions, and prove the Laguerre remainder sign; the global monotonicity-margin sign is rejected by the Xi Lehmer counterexample.
rows.proof_boundary: Direct Laguerre control remains open; the rejected stronger sign is not needed for the corrected C1 transversality route.
